topic: Fan-out deep review lineage gpt55r2-c-5 for MCP server infrastructure scope C

findingDetails.title: Documented tcp:// IPC override is split between incompatible endpoint resolvers
findingDetails.claim: When SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR is a tcp:// endpoint, the MCP context server path-resolves it as a filesystem directory while launcher/CLI code uses the raw TCP endpoint and the HF model-server resolver can also consume that same TCP endpoint, so the documented TCP IPC mode cannot route clients to the MCP daemon reliably.

results.summary: Scope C explicitly includes daemon IPC and CLI front door code; the tcp:// endpoint path is a daemon lifecycle/IPC routing defect.
No checklist.md exists in this lineage scope; evidence review used the scope spec only.

traceabilityChecks.summary: Core traceability partially covered in this single-iteration fan-out lineage.

noveltyJustification: One new endpoint-routing finding was found by comparing the documented scope against daemon IPC path resolution, launcher client resolution, model-server endpoint resolution, and CLI call flow.
ruledOut: No P0 recorded: the issue blocks documented tcp IPC availability but does not prove data loss, privilege escalation, or destructive mutation by itself.

packets.claim: When SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR is a tcp:// endpoint, the MCP context server path-resolves it as a filesystem directory while launcher/CLI code uses the raw TCP endpoint and the HF model-server resolver can also consume that same TCP endpoint, so the documented TCP IPC mode cannot route clients to the MCP daemon reliably.
packets.counterevidenceSought: Checked startIpcSocketServer tcp support and CLI/launcher resolver behavior; startIpcSocketServer can listen on tcp when handed tcp:// directly, but context-server calls resolveIpcSocketPath first, while the CLI/launcher and HF model-server resolvers preserve the raw tcp:// endpoint.
packets.alternativeExplanation: The tcp:// support could be intended only for the HF model server, but ENV_REFERENCE documents SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR as the daemon IPC socket override and the CLI bridge uses it for mk-spec-memory, so this alternative is rejected.
packets.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if tcp:// is explicitly removed from the daemon IPC contract or separate MCP and HF TCP endpoint variables are enforced with tests proving no client/server mismatch.
